title: Terpsichore flexuosa
intro: Terpsichore flexuosa, also known as Brazilian ginseng or flexuous polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including stress, anxiety, and respiratory issues, due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Terpsichore flexuosa include triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Terpsichore flexuosa belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification places Terpsichore flexuosa within the subfamily Polypodioideae. Terpsichore flexuosa is further classified as a species within the genus Terpsichore, which is a group of climbing ferns. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is often found growing on trees or other vegetation in humid environments.
